title: University of Arizona Launches Largest-Ever Phase 3 Rapamycin Trial: 720 Older Adults Over 6 Years
description: The University of Arizona announced a Phase 3 clinical trial enrolling 720+ adults aged 65+ to determine whether low-dose rapamycin (likely 8mg/week) can prevent the transition to frailty and reduce IL-6 inflammatory markers. Funded by $12M from philanthropist R. Ken Coit, this is the largest rapamycin aging trial ever attempted. Dr. Bonnie LaFleur leads the study, which will run for 6 years (2 years of treatment + 1 year follow-up). Combined with UT Health San Antonio's ARPA-H-funded trial of rapamycin, semaglutide, and dapagliflozin in 700+ participants, 2026 marks a watershed moment for clinical validation of geroprotective drugs in humans.

title: Peter Attia's April Research Roundup: Paternal Exercise Confers Offspring Fitness, ANS Aging Theory
description: Peter Attia's April 2026 'Research Worth Sharing' highlights two significant findings. First, a study showing paternal endurance exercise confers VO2max and metabolic fitness benefits to male offspring via sperm microRNAs and PGC-1alpha -- even when offspring never exercise. Second, a new 'sympathetic-parasympathetic deregulation theory of aging' published in Nature proposing that progressive autonomic nervous system drift toward sympathetic dominance drives mitochondrial dysfunction, epigenetic alterations, and physical/cognitive decline. Both open new avenues for longevity intervention beyond diet and direct exercise.

title: ARKG Surges 10.2% as Genomics and Longevity Biotech Rally Enters Fourth Day
description: ARK Genomic Revolution ETF (ARKG) surged 10.23% on Thursday, leading a fourth consecutive day of gains for longevity and biotech ETFs. IDNA rose 3.48%, HTEC +2.99%, XBI +2.76%, SBIO +2.38%, and IBB +1.14%. The rally continues to rotate away from defensive healthcare (XLV -1.82%, IHI -1.06%) into high-beta genomics and biotech. Over the four-day rally from Monday to Thursday, ARKG has gained roughly 30% while XBI has risen approximately 15%.

title: David Sinclair Teases April 2026 Launch: Lifespan Magazine and Funding Initiative
description: David Sinclair made two April announcements via X: he is launching Lifespan Magazine (@JoinLifespan), billed as 'the #1 podcast' with new episodes in April 2026 focused on evidence-based longevity insights, and he continues to tease a longevity research funding initiative, stating the field is 'starved of funds.' With Life Biosciences' ER-100 Phase 1 trial underway and the sector at $3.74B in Q1 funding, Sinclair is positioning himself at the center of both public education and capital mobilization for aging research.

title: 40Hz Light-Sound Therapy Slows Alzheimer's Decline 84% in Phase 2 Trial
description: Peter Attia highlighted a Phase 2 randomized, double-blind, sham-controlled trial (N=53) of 40Hz gamma-frequency light and sound stimulation for mild-to-moderate Alzheimer's. The non-invasive at-home treatment (1 hour/day for 6 months) showed an 84% slower decline in activities of daily living compared to sham, with excellent safety and >80% daily adherence. While amyloid beta was unchanged and cognitive tests mixed, the ADL improvement was striking. A Phase 3 trial is needed, but this could become a zero-risk adjunct therapy for the 7 million Americans with Alzheimer's.

title: University of Arizona Launches Largest Rapamycin Aging Trial: 720 Participants, $12M Philanthropy
description: The U of A R. Ken Coit College of Pharmacy will begin a Phase 3 clinical trial enrolling 720+ adults 65+ to evaluate whether low-dose rapamycin can prevent frailty transition and reduce IL-6 inflammation. The 6-year study is funded by a $12M gift from alumnus R. Ken Coit.

title: Dr. Kaeberlein and Dr. LaFleur Discuss the Largest-Ever Rapamycin Aging Trial
description: Dr. Kaeberlein interviews Dr. Bonnie LaFleur about the 720-person rapamycin Phase 3 trial. The dosing will likely be 8mg/week based on a 50-person pilot showing no immunosuppressive effects. Co-primary endpoints are frailty transition and IL-6 levels.

title: Peter Attia: Research Worth Sharing, April 2026 Edition
description: Peter Attia covers paternal exercise conferring offspring fitness via sperm microRNAs, 40Hz light-sound therapy slowing Alzheimer's ADL decline by 84%, and a new sympathetic-parasympathetic deregulation theory of aging proposing ANS drift as a root cause of aging.

title: Seragon SRN-901 Peer-Reviewed Publication: 33% Lifespan Extension, Outperforms Rapamycin
description: Seragon published data in Drug Design, Development and Therapy showing SRN-901 extended median remaining lifespan by 33% in 18-month-old mice on Western diet, outperforming rapamycin (+21%), NMN, and NR. Frailty attenuated 70%, tumor incidence reduced 30.5%.

title: Vitamin C Identified as Direct ACSL4 Inhibitor, Reverses Biological Age 3-7 Years in Primates
description: Published in Cell Metabolism, researchers identified 'ferro-aging' via ACSL4 as a conserved primate aging mechanism. 40-month Vitamin C treatment in aged monkeys reversed biological age by 3-7 years on multi-omic clocks, improved brain volume, and enhanced metabolic health.

title: Maryland Becomes First State to Sign Longevity Ready Act Into Law
description: Governor Wes Moore signed the Longevity Ready Maryland Act (HB 278/SB 113), making Maryland the first state to enact legislation specifically addressing the impact of longevity with a 10-year multisector plan.

title: Homoharringtonine Validated as Novel Senolytic: FDA-Approved Drug Extends Mouse Lifespan
description: Researchers screened 2,150 compounds and identified HHT as a potent senolytic that clears senescent cells via HSPA5-mediated apoptosis and extends lifespan in progeroid and aged mice.

title: HMGB1 Box A Gene Therapy Reverses Aging Biomarkers in Monkey Study
description: IV administration of HMGB1 Box A plasmid in perimenopausal monkeys reversed age-related proteomic changes, improved bone health, reduced blood glucose, and decreased weight gain over 56 weeks with no adverse effects.

title: David Sinclair Announces Lifespan Magazine and Podcast Launch for April 2026
description: Harvard professor David Sinclair teased the launch of Lifespan Magazine, a new publication and podcast focused on evidence-based longevity insights and actionable advice, coming April 2026.

title: Longevity Biotech Investment Hits $3.74B in Q1 2026, 2026 Tracking to Beat 2021 Record
description: Q1 2026 saw $3.74B raised across 49 financing events (56% increase over Q1 2025). Analyst Matt Hamilton notes 2026 is tracking to beat 2021's record year, with capital flowing into cellular reprogramming, consumer platforms, cardiometabolic longevity, and manufacturing infrastructure.

title: UT Health San Antonio Multi-Drug Aging Trial: Rapamycin, Semaglutide, and Dapagliflozin
description: Over 700 participants will join a major aging trial testing three FDA-approved drugs to determine if they slow biological aging. NIH-funded Phase 2 rapamycin dosing study (84 adults) runs in parallel.

title: ARPA-H Commits $144M to PROSPR Program for Seven Longevity Research Teams
description: ARPA-H awarded $144M to seven teams (4 academic, 3 biotech) to lay groundwork for Phase 3 aging trials. The program aims to validate surrogate biomarkers and build a pathway to regulatory approval for aging interventions.

title: Bryan Johnson Promotes One-Leg Balance Test as Biological Age Proxy at BI Event
description: Bryan Johnson demonstrated a simple eyes-closed one-leg balance test as a biological age proxy at Business Insider's 'The Long Play' event. Supported by a Mayo Clinic study showing one-leg balance as a valid frailty measure in adults 50+.

title: Bryan Johnson 2026 Blueprint Protocol: Data-Driven Refinements, NMN 6x/Week, Lithium and NDGA
description: Mito Health's comprehensive breakdown of Bryan Johnson's 2026 Blueprint emphasizes data-driven refinements over fixed rules, with every adjustment based on measurable lab results. Key changes: NMN/NR 6 days per week, rapamycin dropped, lithium and NDGA added.

title: IGF-1 Signaling Cannot Rescue Mitochondrial Mutator Mice: New Hierarchy in Longevity Pathways
description: Study reveals reduced IGF-1 signaling fails to extend lifespan in mitochondrial mutator mice, establishing that mitochondrial genome integrity is a prerequisite for IGF-1-mediated longevity benefits.

title: Life Biosciences Secures $80M Series D for First Human Epigenetic Reprogramming Trial
description: Life Biosciences, co-founded by David Sinclair, secured FDA approval for Phase 1 of ER-100, an AAV-delivered OSK gene therapy targeting optic neuropathies through partial epigenetic reprogramming.

title: Rapamycin Review in Frontiers in Aging: Promising but Standardized Human Biomarkers Still Needed
description: Comprehensive review finds low-dose intermittent rapamycin appears safe over one year in healthy adults, but current data do not yet support it as a reliable intervention for extending human lifespan without standardized pharmacodynamic biomarkers.

title: Tau Seeds Spread via Brain Connectivity, Supporting Prion-Like Propagation in Alzheimer's
description: Multimodal integration of tau seed bioactivity data from postmortem brain tissues, genotype, and fMRI reveals tau seeds spread via synaptic connections in individual-specific connectivity patterns, supporting prion-like propagation in Alzheimer's disease.

title: Longevity.Technology Week 14: Can Aging Be Treated Like a Disease?
description: Week 14 news roundup explores the framing of aging as a treatable condition, alongside coverage of Noom acquiring a compounding pharmacy, Annovis Bio's buntanetap advancing to Phase 3, and Q1 2026 funding milestones.

title: America's Longevity Crisis: Fortune Analyzes the 12.4-Year Healthspan Gap
description: With the oldest boomers turning 80 in 2026, the US faces a 12.4-year gap between lifespan and healthspan. Only 5% of homes have basic accessibility features and 80% of older households lack resources for long-term care.

title: USPTO Publishes AI-Driven Senolytic Compound Patent Screening 800K+ Molecules
description: Patent application US20260097022A1 discloses AI neural networks trained on 2,352 compounds to predict senolytic activity across 800K+ molecules, with compounds binding Bcl-2 proteins to reduce senescent cell burden.

title: Senolytics Pipeline 2026: Field Splits Into Academic, Biotech, and Local Delivery Lanes
description: Comprehensive review finds human proof-of-biology exists for senolytics, but the field is splitting into academic first-gen, biotech pipeline, and local delivery approaches. Nature Aging compared 21 senolytic agents.

title: Longevity Sector Valued at $23.2B in 2026, Projected $34.8B by 2030
description: The longevity biotech sector stands at $23.2B in 2026 and is projected to reach $34.82B by 2030 at an 11% CAGR, driven by record Q1 funding and expanding clinical pipelines.

title: On Aging 2026 Conference Opens April 20 in Atlanta: Largest US Aging Event
description: The ASA's On Aging 2026 conference takes place April 20-23 in Atlanta, themed 'The Power of Belonging' and focusing on amplifying voices and advancing justice in aging. Largest US multidisciplinary aging event.
